reviews.review_description: I like the concept the only thing needed here is solo rooms or a way to open desktop/laptop screens and work prior to meetups. Would love to not have to use horizon or immerse to do laptop work and then switch over to this. Maybe that is in the professional plan?
Offers some great productivity tools and avatars are really realistic

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback!

reviews.review_description: Using VR for professional collaboration in coaching, training and consulting I have tested plenty of VR platforms. Arthur is by far the most flexible platform, very stable even with larger groups and has an excellent support team. Strong recommendation!

I've been using arthur as a go-to for my mood boards and inspirational art room. Arthur even supports dropping 3D files into the room which I've been the most fond of.  Having fun diving deep into exploring VR and the possibilities here are endless.

Just kept sending me in loops and coming up with error messages.
Waste of time

The server program is too poor. I cannot even register an account. Why did you start your business now?

Error
Please try again. 

Thanks, I never come back.

reviews.review_title: Another VR work from home or whatever.

reviews.review_description: A good experience with the app, very stable than most other VR apps, connecting people from far away. Avatars are good gives the impression of meeting the person in actual
Had a very good experience, very stable VR conferences, and features are very interactive

I'm so impressed with all the productivity features and huge spaces that Arthur offers. 
We had about 50 people in the experience, and it felt so real! 
After comparing Arthur with the other players on the market, the choice was just obvious.

While you wait an incredibly long time for this program to load, you need to stay perfectly still, because the interface moves with the headset. 

I only installed the demo version. Right in the demo, they tell you that you're using an old version. 

Why.

Why would you try to sell something by showing the user a less-good product? And this is definitely less good. Not many features to try, fairly low res, controls not easy to use, and if it's possible to change ones height, it's not obvious how. This is entirely designed for someone who is standing, which is ableist and just plain silly for work meetings.

I would have given it one star, but it does have potential. It's absolutely not even close to usable for it's intended purpose yet, though.
The photo-realistic avatar makes me feel like we are all in the same room. The productivity tools make it easy to collaborate on work topics!

reviews.review_title: Thanks for the feedback I got
reviews.review_description: I don’t know what to do it

I know quite a fews professional Social VR solutions and Arthur is definitely one of the best. What I like most about it is the ease with which you can upload and use your own content. Also, the in-world building capabilities are outstanding: on the tool table you find primitives as well as 3D icons to start customizing the well-designed default rooms Arthur provides. I can't wait to see what these guys do in future. Keep up the good work!

Arthur provides our remote team with a great way to collaborate without any geographical constraints. Unlike Zoom or other video conferencing solutions, Arthur replicates the real world with photorealistic avatars and 3D images. I would recommend Arthur to any remote or hybrid business to cure employees' Zoom fatigue and increase productivity and collaboration.

Ehhh... Could be better. Nice visuals but other than that not very useful when compared to other productivity apps. Its somewhat glitchy. It looks like the developers put form over function. You can spwan white boards and move furniture around. Whats the use of a productiviy app that does not have much functionality?
